analysis.object: T920: The Tropical SAT Solver depth analysis

classical_sat.description: Classical SAT: find assignment maximizing satisfied clauses. NP-complete. Search is EML-inf.

tropical_sat.description: Tropical SAT: max-plus optimization. Each clause becomes a tropical polynomial. FINDING the tropical optimum = max-plus LP = polynomial time.

tropical_in_p.description: Tropical SAT is in P (max-plus LP is polynomial). Classical SAT is NP-complete. IF classical SAT reduces to tropical SAT via descent, P=NP.

descent_complexity.description: Critical question: does Berkovich descent from tropical to classical PRESERVE complexity? If descent increases complexity (tropical P -> classical NP), P≠NP.
descent_complexity.reason: Descent from tropical to classical increases complexity

descent_kills_reduction.description: T815 showed: Berkovich descent preserves spectral GAPS. But descent from tropical SAT to classical SAT INCREASES complexity (the EML-2 tropical solution doesn't directly give an EML-inf classical solution).
descent_kills_reduction.reason: Descent increases complexity: tropical P ≠ classical P

complexity_gap_from_descent.description: The complexity gap between tropical SAT (EML-2) and classical SAT (EML-inf) IS the P≠NP gap. Descent cannot cross from EML-2 to EML-inf without increasing resource.

t920_theorem.description: T920: Tropical SAT is in P (EML-2). Classical SAT is NP-complete (EML-inf). Berkovich descent from tropical to classical INCREASES complexity (EML-2 -> EML-inf). The descent complexity gap = P≠NP gap. T920: tropical SAT gives a complexity-separation argument.
t920_theorem.reason: Tropical SAT in P; descent gap = P≠NP
